skill.name: Azure.ResourceManager.Redis (.NET)
skill.description: A skill for working with Azure Redis Cache using the Azure.ResourceManager.Redis .NET SDK. Provides guidance on creating, configuring, and managing Azure Redis instances through code, including best practices for connection strings, firewall rules, and clustering configurations.
skill.summary: Azure Redis Cache management skill for .NET developers using Azure SDK
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: This is a prompt-only skill containing only documentation in SKILL.md (362 lines). No executable code was scanned. Static analysis found no suspicious patterns, dangerous code patterns, or risk factors. The skill provides guidance on Azure Redis Cache management using the .NET Azure SDK - a legitimate development tool.

content.value_statement: This skill helps .NET developers create and manage Azure Redis Cache instances programmatically using the Azure.ResourceManager.Redis SDK, eliminating manual portal configuration and enabling infrastructure-as-code practices.
actual_capabilities: Create new Azure Redis Cache instances with custom configurations
Configure Redis firewall rules and network settings
Set up Redis clustering and scaling options
Manage connection strings and access keys
Query Redis instance status and metrics
limitations: Does not execute actual Azure operations - provides guidance only
Requires valid Azure subscription and credentials for real operations
Limited to features available in Azure.ResourceManager.Redis SDK
Does not cover Redis client-side implementation details

use_cases.title: Infrastructure Automation
use_cases.description: DevOps engineers automating Redis infrastructure provisioning in CI/CD pipelines

use_cases.title: Application Development
use_cases.description: Developers integrating Azure Redis into .NET applications with proper configuration

use_cases.title: Cloud Migration
use_cases.description: Teams migrating from on-premise Redis to Azure Cloud Cache solutions

prompt_templates.title: Create Basic Redis Instance
prompt_templates.prompt: How do I create an Azure Redis Cache instance using the .NET Azure SDK with basic tier and default settings?

prompt_templates.title: Configure Redis with Firewall Rules
prompt_templates.prompt: Show me how to create a Redis cache with custom firewall rules to allow specific IP addresses using Azure.ResourceManager.Redis.

prompt_templates.title: Set Up Redis Clustering
prompt_templates.prompt: How do I configure Redis clustering with premium tier using the .NET SDK? Include cluster configuration and shard settings.

prompt_templates.title: Manage Redis Keys and Connections
prompt_templates.prompt: What is the proper way to regenerate Redis access keys and get connection strings using the Azure SDK for .NET?

output_examples.input: Create a premium tier Redis cache with clustering enabled
output: Sku = new RedisCacheSku { Name = RedisCacheSkuName.Premium, Family = RedisCacheSkuFamily.P, Capacity = 2 },

output_examples.input: How do I add firewall rules to allow my app service?

best_practices: Use Managed Identity for authentication instead of connection strings when possible
Always enable SSL/TLS for Redis connections in production environments
Configure appropriate TTL values for cached data to prevent stale data issues
anti_patterns: Hardcoding Redis connection strings in source code - use Azure Key Vault instead
Using basic tier for production workloads requiring high availability
Ignoring Redis maxmemory policy settings leading to eviction issues

faq.question: What Azure SDK package do I need?
faq.answer: Install the Azure.ResourceManager.Redis NuGet package in your .NET project.

faq.question: Which Redis tier should I choose?
faq.answer: Basic for development, Standard for production with replication, Premium for clustering and high availability.

faq.question: How do I handle connection string security?
faq.answer: Store connection strings in Azure Key Vault and reference them via Managed Identity.

faq.question: Can I resize my Redis cache after creation?
faq.answer: Yes, you can scale up or down within the same tier family using the Update operation.

faq.question: What is the difference between Basic and Premium tier?
faq.answer: Basic is single node, Premium adds clustering, persistence, and higher throughput.

faq.question: How do I monitor Redis performance?
faq.answer: Use Azure Monitor metrics for memory, CPU, connected clients, and cache hits/misses.
